content:
Have you ever wondered why it’s hot in summer and cold in winter? It’s all because of how Earth moves around the Sun! Imagine Earth as a big spinning top that slowly goes around the Sun once every year. But here’s the tricky part: Earth is not standing straight up—it’s tilted a little bit, like when you lean to one side.

Because of this tilt, different parts of Earth get different amounts of sunlight at different times of the year. When your part of Earth is tilted toward the Sun, you get more sunlight and it feels warm—that’s summer! When your part is tilted away from the Sun, you get less sunlight and it feels colder—that’s winter.

So, the seasons change because Earth is spinning, moving around the Sun, and leaning as it goes. Isn’t it cool how Earth’s tilt helps make the seasons?
